food_subgroup: Mixed grilled meats platter with sides

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Italian grigliata mista. FOODON_03315595 (mixed meat dish) is the closest defensible parent category covering a platter of multiple grilled meats.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Meat, grilled) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a mixed Italian grill platter combining beef, chicken, pork chop, and sausages with potato and salad sides.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for Italian mixed grill platter as a composite dish entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One sharing platter for 4–5 persons, estimated at approximately 1,200 g total (roughly 240–300 g per person), comprising assorted grilled meats, potato wedges, and garden salad.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — multiple entries: Beef ribeye grilled (ID 174032), Chicken thigh grilled (ID 331960), Pork chop grilled (ID 167903), Pork sausage cooked (ID 167904), Potato wedges baked (ID 170438), Mixed garden salad (ID 169248); values weighted and blended by estimated component proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for grigliata mista as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by blending USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods entries for each component: Angus ribeye grilled (~30% by weight), boneless chicken thigh grilled (~25%), Iberico-style pork chop grilled (~20%), Italian pork sausage cooked (~10%), potato wedges baked (~10%), garden salad (~5%). Per-100g values represent the weighted composite. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021; analogous estimate based on grilled mixed meats (GI ~0–10) combined with potato wedges (GI ~55) and salad (GI ~15), weighted by component proportion
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for grigliata mista as a composite dish. GI estimated as weighted average: grilled beef/chicken/pork (GI ~0, protein-fat dominant, ~65% of edible weight), Italian pork sausage cooked (GI ~28, per Sydney GI Database), potato wedges baked (GI ~55, per Sydney GI Database), garden salad (GI ~15, per Sydney GI Database). Weighted composite GI ≈ 45. GL per serving calculated as: (45 × 86.4 g carbohydrate per 1200 g serving) / 100 = 38.9, rounded to 39.0. gi_category set to 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from sausages and seasoned meats
hypertension.guidance: The platter contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g, driven primarily by Italian pork sausages and seasoned marinades; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and request reduced-salt preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from potato wedges offset by high protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: The composite GI of approximately 45 (low) is moderated by the dominant protein and fat content of the grilled meats, but the glycemic load per full serving is substantial (~39); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit their portion to one-quarter of the platter and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from red meat, pork chop, and sausages
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The platter provides approximately 3.8 g saturated fat and 72 mg cholesterol per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally, favour leaner cuts such as chicken thigh, and avoid the sausage component which contributes the highest saturated fat and sodium load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grigliata Mista, meaning 'mixed grill' in Italian, is a substantial sharing platter of assorted grilled meats originating from Italian culinary tradition and served at Italian restaurants in Singapore such as Ricciotti at The Riverwalk. The platter typically comprises Premium Angus ribeye steak, Diavola-style boneless chicken thighs (marinated with chilli and herbs), Iberico pork chop, Italian pork sausages, potato wedges, and a garden salad, designed to serve 4–5 persons. All meats are cooked over a grill or chargrill, producing characteristic smoky, charred exterior flavours with juicy, tender interiors. The ribeye contributes rich umami and marbled fat; the chicken thighs offer a spiced, slightly smoky flavour; the Iberico pork chop provides a nutty, sweet pork flavour from the acorn-fed breed; and the Italian sausages add herbed, fennel-forward seasoning. Nutritionally, the composite dish is high in protein (approximately 18.5 g per 100 g) and moderate in fat (10.8 g per 100 g), with a low glycemic index of approximately 45 driven by the protein-fat dominant meat components. The potato wedges contribute the primary carbohydrate load. Sodium is moderate to high at approximately 310 mg per 100 g, largely from the sausages and seasoned marinades. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following halal dietary requirements due to the pork components. Clinically, individuals with hypertension should monitor sodium intake, those with Type 2 Diabetes should manage portion size due to the glycemic load from potato wedges, and those with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of saturated fat from red meat and sausages.
common_user_queries: Grilled meat platter with ribeye and pork

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for grigliata mista as a composite restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated by blending USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for each identified component at estimated proportions. GI is an analogous weighted estimate using Sydney GI Database values for component foods. Confidence is medium rather than low because all component sources are approved and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Meat and alternatives' as the dish is predominantly a grilled meat platter. 2. Serving size estimated at 1,200 g total for 4–5 persons based on typical restaurant portion norms for a multi-protein sharing platter. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as vitamin D is not reliably reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods component entries used to construct this composite dish estimate; the source database does not provide consistent vitamin D values across all component foods (beef ribeye, chicken thigh, pork chop, pork sausage, potato wedges, garden salad). 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as vitamin E is not consistently reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods component entries used for this composite dish; the source database does not provide vitamin E values for all component foods at the required specificity. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as selenium is not consistently reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods component entries used for this composite dish; the source database does not provide selenium values for all component foods at the required specificity. 6. The dish contains pork (Iberico pork chop and Italian pork sausages) and is therefore not halal. 7. Allergen note: Italian pork sausages may contain gluten in casings or seasoning blends; mustard may be present in marinades. 8. GL per serving = (GI 45 × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (45 × 86.4) / 100 ≈ 39.0, where carbohydrate per serving = 7.2 g/100g × 1200 g = 86.4 g. 9. The cuisine_tags from the input (Pasta, Pizza) are not applicable to this dish and have been excluded from the output record. 10. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03315595 and FoodEx2 A0CKP are closest_parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for this composite Italian mixed grill dish.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is canonical English 'Grigliata Mista (Italian Mixed Grill Platter)'. Local names provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi satisfying the Italian cuisine served in Singapore context. Romanized Italian name preserved.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros present and numeric: energy_kcal 195.0, protein_g 18.5, fat_g 10.8, carbohydrate_g 7.2. Source is USDA FoodData Central with named component entries and transparent blending methodology. No zero macros requiring justification.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.9, sodium_mg 310.0, saturated_fat_g 3.8, sugar_g 1.0 — all present as numeric values, plausible for a mixed grilled meat and potato platter.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 45 (numeric, >0), gi_category 'low' (correct per ≤55 threshold), glycemic_load_per_serving 39.0, gi_source present citing University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 AJCN. Analogous estimate basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03315595 (mixed meat dish) as closest_parent with gap note. FoodEx2 A0CKP (Meat, grilled) as closest_parent with gap note. SNOMED CT null with match_type 'none' and gap note. No codes invented. All match_type values are within the allowed set (exact, closest_parent, none).

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is approximately 1,350 characters, well above the 200-character minimum. Covers what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture of each component, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate, GI/GL and portion), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate, saturated fat and cholesterol). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: Flat aliases list contains 16 entries covering English variants, Italian romanized name, Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. aliases_by_language is fully consistent with flat aliases — every string in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with component-blended USDA nutrient values, weighted GI estimate, multilingual names for Italian dish served in Singapore context, ontology with closest_parent matches, and full health context.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases array — confirmed all strings match verbatim. Verified GI category threshold (GI 45 ≤ 55 = low). Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified ontology match_type values are within allowed set. Verified no zero macros without justification.

Iteration 3: Resolved g3_numeric_hygiene REJECT from iteration 2. Expanded notes items 3, 4, and 5 to explicitly name USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods as the source database and state that vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, and selenium_mcg are -1 because the source database does not provide consistent or complete values for these nutrients across all component foods used in the composite estimate. All three -1 sentinel values now have fully documented missing-data basis referencing the specific source database, satisfying g3_numeric_hygiene.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on Q1–Q8. The g3_numeric_hygiene failure from iteration 2 (undocumented -1 values for vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, and selenium_mcg) has been resolved by expanding the notes field to explicitly document the source database and reason for each -1 sentinel value. Grigliata Mista is correctly identified as a mixed Italian grilled meat sharing platter. Nutrient values are transparently estimated from USDA FoodData Central component entries with named proportions. GI is an analogous weighted estimate from Sydney GI Database component values, documented clearly. Multilingual names cover Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for an Italian dish served in Singapore. Ontology uses closest_parent matches with honest gap notes. All three health conditions are present with specific clinical guidance. LLM description exceeds 200 characters with full contextual coverage. Aliases are consistent between flat list and structured object.
